rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aerobic oxidative C=C bond cleavage/oxyfunctionalization of 1,1,2-triaryl alkenes to α,α-diaryl ketones using O2 as the oxygen source
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the triaryl alkene to a vinyl/alkene radical cation (Ep,a ~0.918 V observed by CV under O2), which is then trapped by molecular oxygen

key_intermediates.name: peroxy/dioxetane intermediate from O2 addition to radical cation

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the triaryl alkene to its radical cation; molecular O2 (incorporated as shown by 18O2 labeling) adds to the radical cation to give a peroxy/dioxetane species that fragments/rearranges to the α,α-diaryl ketone. Water is not the oxygen source (no reaction under Ar with H2 18O).

probe_reagents.name: 18O2 (isotopically labeled dioxygen)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2a shows 18O incorporation by HRMS (found 275.1322 for C20H17 18O), confirming O2 as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O (isotopically labeled water)
probe_reagents.observation: Under Ar with various equivalents of H2 18O (0.5–2.0 equiv, or 4:1 CH3CN/H2O), no reaction (N.R.) — water is not the O source

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry under N2 vs O2
probe_reagents.observation: An additional oxidation feature at ~0.918 V appears under O2 atmosphere, consistent with an O2-involved oxidation event

notes: The 18O2 vs H2 18O double-labeling cleanly distinguishes O2 (operative) from H2O (inoperative) as the oxygen source. CV under O2 suggests interaction between O2 and the oxidized substrate/electrode. No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT) or DFT reported on these pages, so the exact peroxy intermediate (open peroxy vs 1,2-dioxetane) is not directly distinguished.
